meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't collaborate. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Master Rapid Collaboration for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with fragmented workflows. Use this intermediate guide to deploy a SaaS starter kit using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Collaboration bottlenecks kill freelance margins. By deploying a standardized SaaS starter kit, you unify your architecture, automate boilerplate, and synchronize team workflows. Using Antigravity for structural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you convert chaotic development cycles into a streamlined, repeatable asset that impresses clients and accelerates delivery.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: the client is waiting, the codebase is a mess of spaghetti, and your collaborator is pushing code that breaks your local environment. The friction of manual configuration and mismatched dependencies is the silent killer of your freelance reputation. It feels like you’re building on quicksand, where every feature request turns into a three-hour debugging nightmare.

But the tide is turning. By adopting a hardened, battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you stop reinventing the wheel and start building the engine. We’re moving beyond manual setup into the era of AI-orchestrated development, where Antigravity provides the structural backbone and Claude Code acts as your high-velocity co-pilot to ensure every line of code is production-ready.

steps.title: Architect the Unified Foundation
steps.description: Establish a base repository that enforces strict TypeScript standards and shared environment configurations to eliminate 'it works on my machine' errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular SaaS boilerplate structure using Next.js 15, Tailwind CSS, and Drizzle ORM. Ensure all environment variables are schema-validated with Zod.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure with strict TypeScript configurations and Drizzle schema setup'

steps.title: Automate Authentication & RBAC
steps.description: Integrate a robust authentication layer that supports multi-tenant collaboration out of the box, ensuring your client projects are secure from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Clerk authentication with multi-tenant organization support. Define middleware to enforce role-based access control for admin and user routes.

steps.title: Implement Collaborative State Management
steps.description: Use TanStack Query to synchronize client state across the frontend, ensuring real-time data consistency between collaborators.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup TanStack Query with a centralized API client factory that handles request retries, caching, and error logging for all CRUD operations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate TanStack Query and create a reusable API factory hook'

steps.title: Standardize Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: Create a CI/CD workflow that runs automated tests and linting before every push, preventing breaking changes from reaching production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, type-checking, and unit tests on every pull request targeting the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate GitHub Actions CI workflow for Next.js build and test'

steps.title: Deploy Documentation-as-Code
steps.description: Embed technical documentation directly into the repo so collaborators can onboard without manual hand-holding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md and a CONTRIBUTING.md file that outlines the folder architecture and deployment steps for this SaaS starter kit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create comprehensive project documentation and setup guide'

pro_tips: Always use a 'schema-first' approach; define your database schema in Zod before writing any frontend code to ensure type safety across the stack.
Utilize git hooks (Husky) to enforce linting; prevent bad code from ever leaving the local development environment.
Keep your starter kit 'headless'—don't hardcode business logic; instead, build reusable UI components in a private npm registry or local workspace.
Implement a 'feature flag' system early, allowing you to deploy unfinished code to production without exposing it to the end user.
Treat your starter kit as a product; version it using SemVer so you can track improvements and roll back if a new dependency breaks your flow.
content.next_steps: After completing your starter kit, move to building a CLI tool that clones this repo with pre-filled environment variables to reduce your project setup time from hours to minutes.
content.conversion_block: You now have the blueprint to stop the collaboration madness and start shipping enterprise-grade SaaS products at scale. Stop fighting your tools and start mastering them. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
